differences.title: 1. ROUTING
differences.leftDesc: Nested routes with layout inheritance, ideal for complex UIs and shared states.
differences.rightDesc: App router for nested routes, pages router for static/SSR. Flexible but can be complex.

differences.title: 2. DATA LOADING
differences.leftDesc: Loads data on the server via route loaders, ensures data before render.
differences.rightDesc: Server/Client fetching (getStaticProps, getServerSideProps, RSC). Offers choice.

differences.title: 3. MUTATIONS
differences.leftDesc: Dedicated action functions handle form submissions and mutations server-side efficiently.
differences.rightDesc: API routes for REST, Server Actions for mutations. Can be separate concerns.

differences.title: 4. ERROR HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Explicit error boundaries for routes, provides graceful fallback UI.
differences.rightDesc: Handles errors at page or component level, relies on React error boundaries.

differences.title: 5. CLIENT BUNDLE
differences.leftDesc: Loads only essential JS for interactivity, prioritizing performance and load times.
differences.rightDesc: Depends on features used, potential for larger bundles with many client components.

differences.title: 6. FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Leverages web platform features like Fetch API and HTTP caching.
differences.rightDesc: Deep integration with React, Server Components, and Next.js specific features.

differences.title: 7. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Clear separation of server and client state, relies on data loaders and context.
differences.rightDesc: Built-in context API, external libraries (Zustand, Redux) common for complex client state.
